title: Portfolio Analysis Configuration Schema (V2)
description: Configuration schema for the V2 two-analysis architecture. Supports agentic-readiness (ARA), modernization (MOD), or full (both) analysis types. Breaking change from V1 — old configs require migration (goal → analysis_type, goal_context → context, deployment-cicd → deployment-config).

portfolio_name.description: Name of the portfolio being analyzed

analysis_type.description: Determines which analysis TDs to run. 'agentic-readiness' runs ARA only, 'modernization' runs MOD only, 'bpmn-opportunity' runs BPMN opportunity analysis only (requires .bpmn files), 'full' runs all analyses in parallel.

context.description: Optional free-text context for framing recommendations. Passed to all TDs (ARA, MOD, Portfolio ARA, Portfolio MOD) as general framing context.
examples: Building a customer support agent for order and inventory data
Migrating off Oracle to reduce licensing costs by 60%
Evaluating portfolio for both agentic integration and cloud modernization

agent_scope.description: Scope of agent integration — affects conditional BLOCKER evaluation in ARA. ARA-only: the Power passes this only to ARA ATX configs and ignores it for MOD configs. Defaults to 'read-only' if not provided.

transformation_definitions.description: Names of the AWS Transform custom transformation definitions. The four core TDs (agentic_readiness, modernization, portfolio_agentic_readiness, portfolio_modernization) are required. The optional portfolio_bridge TD cross-references ARA and MOD portfolio reports when analysis_type is 'full'.

agentic_readiness.description: Transformation definition name for individual ARA (Agentic Readiness Analysis) per repo

modernization.description: Transformation definition name for individual MOD (Modernization Analysis) per repo

portfolio_agentic_readiness.description: Transformation definition name for portfolio-level ARA aggregation

portfolio_modernization.description: Transformation definition name for portfolio-level MOD aggregation

portfolio_bridge.description: Transformation definition name for the bridge TD that cross-references ARA and MOD portfolio reports. Only used when analysis_type is 'full'.

bpmn_opportunity.description: Transformation definition name for BPMN agentic opportunity analysis per repo. Requires .bpmn files in the repository.

portfolio_bpmn_opportunity.description: Transformation definition name for portfolio-level BPMN opportunity aggregation. Optional.

preferences.description: Global transformation preferences using flat prefer/avoid arrays. MOD-only: the Power passes these only to MOD ATX configs (individual and portfolio) and ignores them for ARA configs. Per-repo preferences merge with (and can override) these global preferences.

prefer.description: Technologies, patterns, or approaches to prefer in recommendations (e.g., eks, aurora, bedrock, rest-api)

avoid.description: Technologies, patterns, or approaches to avoid in recommendations (e.g., serverless, self-managed-kafka, microservices-decomposition)

repositories.description: List of repositories to include in the portfolio analysis

name.description: Service/repository name (must be unique within portfolio)

path.description: Absolute or relative path to the repository root directory. If repository_url is provided and this path doesn't exist, it will be cloned here

priority.description: Business priority for this service (P0=Critical, P1=High, P2=Medium)

context.description: Optional free-text context about this repository (e.g., 'Main order processing service, handles 80% of traffic')

preferences.description: Per-repo transformation preferences. MOD-only. Merged with global preferences — per-repo avoid overrides global prefer for conflicts.

prefer.description: Technologies, patterns, or approaches to prefer for this repo

avoid.description: Technologies, patterns, or approaches to avoid for this repo

repo_type.description: Optional override for repository type classification. If not provided, the agent auto-detects during discovery.

service_archetype.description: Optional override for service archetype classification. MOD-only: calibrates scoring on architecture-sensitive questions (INF-Q3, INF-Q4, APP-Q3, APP-Q4) so services whose correct design is synchronous and stateless are not penalized for lacking async infrastructure they do not need. Only applies when repo_type is 'application'. If not provided, the MOD TD auto-detects during discovery.

tags.description: Optional tags for categorization (e.g., frontend, backend, data, ml, monolith)

repository_url.description: Optional Git repository URL. If provided and path doesn't exist, Kiro will clone the repository automatically

report_path.description: Optional explicit path to the analysis report (if not in standard location)

agent_scope.description: Optional per-repo override for agent scope. ARA-only. If set, overrides the portfolio-level agent_scope for this repo. Use to set infrastructure-only repos to read-only while application repos are write-enabled.

downstream_dependencies.description: Optional list of downstream systems that this repository's BPMN processes depend on. When provided and analysis_type includes bpmn-opportunity, the orchestrator runs ARA on each dependency and passes the results to the BPMN Opportunity TD for cross-referencing (populates the 'declared' section of the dependency report). Each entry maps a BPMN-inferred dependency to the code repository where that system lives.

name.description: Human-readable name of the downstream system (should match or be recognizable from BPMN service task references)

path.description: Local path to the downstream system's code repository (for ARA to scan)

repository_url.description: Optional Git URL. If provided and path doesn't exist, Kiro clones it before running ARA.

context.description: Optional context about this dependency (e.g., 'REST API for credit score lookups, Java/Spring Boot')

dependency_overrides.description: Optional manual dependency declarations (for cases where auto-detection fails)

description.description: Optional description of the dependency relationship
